visual_findings.control_map_diagnostic: The diagnostic image is the expected RGB BAE normal map with smooth surface-direction colors over the face, hair, shoulders, and background. It shows no unexpected generated portrait content.
visual_findings.face_realism: Pass. The generated portrait has coherent facial structure, stable nose/cheek/jaw planes, aligned lips, and no melted geometry.
visual_findings.eye_quality: Pass. Eyes are aligned, symmetrical enough for a local smoke sample, and free of cross-eye or severe iris artifacts.
visual_findings.skin_texture: Pass with notes. Skin is plausible but noticeably beauty-polished on cheeks and forehead, with limited pore breakup.
visual_findings.hair_realism: Pass. Hair mass, flyaway strands, and edge silhouette are coherent without obvious melting.
visual_findings.pose_and_anatomy: Pass for the visible head-and-shoulders crop. Neck, shoulders, and collar geometry are coherent; hands and full-body anatomy are not visible and therefore not certified.
visual_findings.clothing_and_fabric: Pass with notes. Blazer reads cleanly and the shirt collar is coherent, but shirt/fabric detail is slightly soft.
visual_findings.background_lighting_camera: Pass. Background is coherent and lighting is believable for a portrait setup.
visual_findings.control_map_leakage: Pass. No visible rainbow normal-map colors, purple/green/blue stains, or surface-vector artifacts appear in the generated portrait.
visual_findings.prompt_compliance: Pass with notes. The output is a hyperrealistic editorial portrait and appears to use normal-map guidance for stable facial planes, but this one sample does not prove broader surface-shape robustness.

notes: This proves local normal ControlNet Union model load, SetUnionControlNetType=normal routing, and bounded generation for one head-and-shoulders sample.
The sample does not certify hands, full-body anatomy, multi-sample robustness, or target-runtime behavior.
Slight beauty-polished skin and soft shirt texture remain as quality notes.
promotion_boundary: Local smoke generation plus strict visual QA only. Final normal-lane certification and target-runtime EC2 proof remain unclaimed.
next_action: Use this as first local normal ControlNet generation proof. The non-Canny control generation model gap is now locally smoke-covered for depth, lineart, OpenPose, and normal; next work should be robustness/certification sampling or target-runtime proof only when intentionally selected.
